name: Arma 3
author: ops@refx.example
description: Bohemia's military sandbox. SteamCMD-installed dedicated server with mod and mission support.

installScript.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -uo pipefail
# --- ReFx SteamCMD update safety (injected) --------------------------------
# Guards every Steam update: a stuck/partial SteamCMD run (state 0x6, "Missing
# file permissions", ENOSPC, transient failure) must NOT be reported as success
# — that strands the server on a stale build ("wrong build" on join). We key off
# the app's manifest StateFlags: exactly "4" == fully installed & current.
REFX_APPID="233780"
refx_steam_manifest() { echo "/mnt/server/steamapps/appmanifest_${1}.acf"; }
refx_steam_installed() { local m; m="$(refx_steam_manifest "$1")"; [ -f "$m" ] && grep -Eq '"StateFlags"[[:space:]]+"4"' "$m"; }
refx_steam_heal() { # drop a stale/partial manifest so this update re-acquires cleanly
  local m; m="$(refx_steam_manifest "$1")"
  if [ -f "$m" ] && ! refx_steam_installed "$1"; then
    echo "[refx] steam app $1: a previous install left a stale manifest (not fully-installed) — clearing so this update re-acquires the build cleanly."
    rm -f "$m" 2>/dev/null || true
  fi
  rm -rf "/mnt/server/steamapps/downloading/$1" "/mnt/server/steamapps/temp/$1" 2>/dev/null || true
}
refx_steam_verify() { # HARD-FAIL if the app is not fully installed after the update
  if refx_steam_installed "$1"; then
    echo "[refx] steam app $1 is on the current build (SteamCMD confirmed fully installed)."
    return 0
  fi
  echo "[refx] ERROR: SteamCMD did not finish updating app $1 — the game files were NOT"
  echo "[refx] changed, so the server is still on its previous build and clients may see a"
  echo "[refx] 'wrong build' / version-mismatch error on join."
  echo "[refx][diag] install uid:gid = $(id -u 2>/dev/null):$(id -g 2>/dev/null)"
  df -h /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][disk] /' || true
  ls -ln /mnt/server 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/[refx][diag] /' | head -20 || true
  # Clear the stale manifest so the NEXT update self-heals (clean re-acquire).
  rm -f "$(refx_steam_manifest "$1")" 2>/dev/null || true
  echo "[refx] Cleared the stale manifest — run Update again and it will re-download the"
  echo "[refx] current build. Your world/config under the data dir are untouched."
  exit 1
}
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
refx_steam_heal "$REFX_APPID"

mkdir -p /mnt/server
cd /mnt/server
# Locate or bootstrap steamcmd (the parkervcp/steamcmd image does NOT ship it
# on PATH; eggs are expected to fetch it themselves).
if command -v steamcmd >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  STEAMCMD=steamcmd
else
  echo "[steam] bootstrapping steamcmd"
  mkdir -p /mnt/server/steamcmd
  curl -sSL https://steamcdn-a.akamaihd.net/client/installer/steamcmd_linux.tar.gz | tar zxf - -C /mnt/server/steamcmd
  chmod +x /mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh /mnt/server/steamcmd/linux32/steamcmd 2>/dev/null || true
  STEAMCMD=/mnt/server/steamcmd/steamcmd.sh
fi
# The HOST game-download account uses a node-level home (REFX_NODE_STEAM_HOME,
# mounted by the agent) so its Steam Guard machine-auth persists across servers
# — a code is only needed once per node. Each account gets its OWN subdir, so
# switching the game-download account never reuses or refreshes a previous
# account's cached Steam session. Falls back to a per-server home on older agents.
SERVER_HOME=/mnt/server/.steamhome
mkdir -p "$SERVER_HOME"
if [ -n "${REFX_NODE_STEAM_HOME:-}" ]; then
  GAME_HOME="$REFX_NODE_STEAM_HOME/${STEAM_GAME_USERNAME:-anonymous}"
else
  GAME_HOME="$SERVER_HOME"
fi
mkdir -p "$GAME_HOME" 2>/dev/null || GAME_HOME="$SERVER_HOME"
# BASE GAME download uses the HOST's game-download account (Admin → Settings →
# Steam). Arma 3's dedicated server (233780) is NOT anonymous.
if [ -n "${STEAM_GAME_USERNAME:-}" ] && [ -n "${STEAM_GAME_PASSWORD:-}" ]; then
  GLOGIN=(+login "$STEAM_GAME_USERNAME" "$STEAM_GAME_PASSWORD")
  [ -n "${STEAM_GAME_GUARD:-}" ] && GLOGIN+=("$STEAM_GAME_GUARD")
else
  GLOGIN=(+login anonymous)
  echo "[steam] No game-download account configured (Admin → Settings → Steam). Arma 3 requires one that owns the game."
fi
# Build ONE steamcmd session: base game update + every Workshop item, so there's
# a single login (avoids Steam login rate limits). On a mods-only Workshop sync
# (REFX_WORKSHOP_SYNC) the base game validation is skipped when already installed.
WS_APP="${WORKSHOP_APP_ID:-107410}"
SC=(+force_install_dir /mnt/server "${GLOGIN[@]}")
if [ "${REFX_WORKSHOP_SYNC:-0}" = "1" ] && { [ -f /mnt/server/arma3server_x64 ] || [ -f /mnt/server/arma3server ]; }; then
  echo "[steam] mods-only sync — skipping base game validation"
else
  SC+=(+app_update 233780 validate)
fi
if [ -n "${WORKSHOP_ITEMS:-}" ]; then
  for id in $WORKSHOP_ITEMS; do SC+=(+workshop_download_item "$WS_APP" "$id" validate); done
fi
SC+=(+quit)
# stdin from /dev/null so a Steam Guard prompt can't hang the install forever —
# it fails fast instead, and the server is marked crashed with this hint.
HOME="$GAME_HOME" "$STEAMCMD" "${SC[@]}" </dev/null || echo "[steam] steamcmd exited with an error. Missing Workshop items are retried below (downloads RESUME where they left off). If the LOGIN itself failed and the account uses Steam Guard, set a fresh EMAIL code in Admin -> Settings -> Steam (mobile 30s codes expire during steamcmd's self-update) and reinstall; the node is remembered after the first success."
# Big Workshop mods (multi-GB terrains like CUP) routinely hit steamcmd's fixed
# download timeout ("ERROR! Timeout downloading item ...") and abort the WHOLE
# session, killing every later +workshop_download_item too. Downloads resume
# where they left off, so retry just the still-missing items (bounded). One
# login per ATTEMPT, not per item, to respect Steam login rate limits.
ws_missing() {
  local out=""
  for id in $WORKSHOP_ITEMS; do
    [ -d "/mnt/server/steamapps/workshop/content/$WS_APP/$id" ] || out="$out $id"
  done
  printf '%s' "$out"
}
if [ -n "${WORKSHOP_ITEMS:-}" ]; then
  for attempt in 1 2 3 4 5; do
    MISSING="$(ws_missing)"
    [ -z "$MISSING" ] && break
    echo "[workshop] resume attempt $attempt/5 for missing item(s):$MISSING"
    RC=(+force_install_dir /mnt/server "${GLOGIN[@]}")
    for id in $MISSING; do RC+=(+workshop_download_item "$WS_APP" "$id" validate); done
    RC+=(+quit)
    HOME="$GAME_HOME" "$STEAMCMD" "${RC[@]}" </dev/null || true
    sleep 3
  done
  MISSING="$(ws_missing)"
  if [ -n "$MISSING" ]; then
    echo "[workshop] ERROR: still missing after the resume attempts:$MISSING"
    echo "[workshop] Very large mods can exceed one install run. Run Update (or Workshop -> Apply) again -"
    echo "[workshop] downloads continue where they left off and complete across runs. Nothing is lost."
  else
    echo "[workshop] all Workshop item(s) downloaded."
  fi
fi
# Post-process Workshop items: lowercase (Linux is case-sensitive), symlink, keys.
if [ -n "${WORKSHOP_ITEMS:-}" ]; then
  for id in $WORKSHOP_ITEMS; do
    src="/mnt/server/steamapps/workshop/content/$WS_APP/$id"
    [ -d "$src" ] || { echo "[workshop] $id not downloaded"; continue; }
    find "$src" -depth | while read -r p; do
      b="$(basename "$p")"; lb="$(printf '%s' "$b" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')"
      [ "$b" = "$lb" ] || { pd="$(dirname "$p")"; [ -e "$pd/$lb" ] || mv "$p" "$pd/$lb"; }
    done
    ln -sfn "steamapps/workshop/content/$WS_APP/$id" "/mnt/server/@ws_$id"
    mkdir -p /mnt/server/keys
    find "$src" -iname '*.bikey' -exec cp -f {} /mnt/server/keys/ \; 2>/dev/null || true
  done
fi
if [ ! -f /mnt/server/arma3server_x64 ] && [ ! -f /mnt/server/arma3server ]; then
  echo "ERROR: Arma 3 server files are missing — set a game-download Steam account (Admin → Settings → Steam) that owns Arma 3, then reinstall."
  exit 1
fi
# Steam API runtime: Arma 3 (a Steam-API game) SIGSEGVs INSTANTLY on launch with
# NO output if it can't dlopen steamclient.so from ~/.steam/sdk{32,64}. steamcmd
# scatters that file across version-dependent locations (its own linuxXX dirs, or
# the login HOME's .steam/.local), so locate it ROBUSTLY by arch and warn LOUDLY
# if missing — a silent miss here is the classic "exited code 139, no .rpt" crash.
mkdir -p /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32 /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64
sc_find() { # $1 = 32|64 — first steamclient.so whose path names that arch
  find "$GAME_HOME" /mnt/server/steamcmd /mnt/server/.steam /root/.steam /root/.local /usr 2>/dev/null \
    -type f -name steamclient.so \( -path "*linux$1*" -o -path "*sdk$1*" \) -print 2>/dev/null | head -n1
}
SC32="$(sc_find 32)"
SC64="$(sc_find 64)"
if [ -n "$SC32" ]; then cp -fv "$SC32" /mnt/server/.steam/sdk32/steamclient.so; else echo "[refx] WARN: 32-bit steamclient.so not found at install time"; fi
if [ -n "$SC64" ]; then
  cp -fv "$SC64" /mnt/server/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so
  # Arma's 64-bit binary also probes next to itself; provide a copy there too.
  cp -f "$SC64" /mnt/server/steamclient.so 2>/dev/null || true
else
  echo "[refx] WARN: 64-bit steamclient.so NOT found at install time. The runtime launcher will try to supply it from the game image; if it still segfaults (code 139), reinstall once steamcmd has fully self-updated."
fi
# Some Steam games refuse to initialise without an appid hint in the working dir.
echo 233780 > /mnt/server/steam_appid.txt

# Seed a minimal valid server.cfg / basic.cfg from the panel variables if absent
# (the server boots into the lobby; admins edit these via the file manager).
if [ ! -f /mnt/server/server.cfg ]; then
  cat > /mnt/server/server.cfg <<CFGEOF
hostname = "${SERVER_NAME:-A ReFx Hosting Arma 3 Server}";
password = "${SERVER_PASSWORD:-}";
passwordAdmin = "${SERVER_PASSWORD:-changeme}";
maxPlayers = ${MAX_PLAYERS:-32};
persistent = 1;
voteThreshold = 0.33;
kickDuplicate = 1;
class Missions {};
CFGEOF
fi
if [ ! -f /mnt/server/basic.cfg ]; then
  cat > /mnt/server/basic.cfg <<BCFGEOF
MaxMsgSend = 128;
MaxSizeGuaranteed = 512;
MaxSizeNonguaranteed = 256;
MinBandwidth = 1048576;
MaxBandwidth = 251658240;
MinErrorToSend = 0.001;
MinErrorToSendNear = 0.01;
BCFGEOF
fi

# Write the ReFx launcher that runs the server and streams its RPT log to the
# panel console (Arma logs detail to the .rpt file, not stdout).
cat > /mnt/server/refx-run.sh <<'RUNEOF'
#!/bin/bash
# ReFx launcher for Arma 3: runs the dedicated server AND streams its RPT log to
# stdout, because Arma writes its detailed output to a timestamped .rpt file (in
# the profiles dir) rather than to stdout. This makes the panel console useful.
set -u
cd /home/container 2>/dev/null || cd "$(dirname "$0")"
PROFILES="$PWD/serverprofile"
mkdir -p "$PROFILES"

# Steam API env: Arma's libsteam_api.so reads these; without them (or without
# steamclient.so reachable) the server SIGSEGVs at init with no output.
export SteamAppId=233780
export STEAMAPPID=233780
export SteamGameId=233780
[ -f "$PWD/steam_appid.txt" ] || echo 233780 > "$PWD/steam_appid.txt" 2>/dev/null || true

# Safety net (runs INSIDE the real game image): guarantee steamclient.so exists in
# ~/.steam/sdk{32,64}. If the install step couldn't supply it, pull a copy off the
# runtime image — this is the layer most likely to have a valid one.
ensure_sc() { # $1 = 32|64
  local dst="$HOME/.steam/sdk$1/steamclient.so"
  [ -f "$dst" ] && return 0
  mkdir -p "$HOME/.steam/sdk$1"
  local src
  src="$(find "$HOME" /usr /opt /root /home 2>/dev/null -type f -name steamclient.so \( -path "*linux$1*" -o -path "*sdk$1*" \) -print 2>/dev/null | head -n1)"
  [ -n "$src" ] && cp -f "$src" "$dst" 2>/dev/null && echo "[refx] supplied steamclient.so (sdk$1) from $src"
}
ensure_sc 32
ensure_sc 64
if [ ! -f "$HOME/.steam/sdk64/steamclient.so" ]; then
  echo "[refx] WARN: no 64-bit steamclient.so available — arma3server_x64 will likely SIGSEGV. Reinstall the server to fetch it."
fi

BIN=./arma3server_x64
[ -x "$BIN" ] || BIN=./arma3server
# Mirror the proven upstream Pterodactyl egg's invocation (bind all, BattlEye path,
# relative profiles). Arma writes its .rpt under the profiles dir.
ARGS=(-ip=0.0.0.0 -port="${SERVER_PORT:-2302}" -config=server.cfg -cfg=basic.cfg -bepath="$PWD" -profiles="$PROFILES" -name=refx)
# Build the -mod list: user-specified MODS plus the enabled Workshop items the
# panel downloaded (symlinked as @ws_<id> by the install step).
MODLIST="${MODS:-}"
for id in ${WORKSHOP_ITEMS:-}; do
  d="@ws_$id"
  [ -e "$d" ] || continue
  case ";$MODLIST;" in *";$d;"*) ;; *) MODLIST="${MODLIST:+$MODLIST;}$d";; esac
done
[ -n "$MODLIST" ] && ARGS+=(-mod="$MODLIST")

SRV=
shutdown() { [ -n "$SRV" ] && kill -TERM "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; }
trap shutdown TERM INT

# Arma needs to find its bundled libsteam_api.so (server dir) AND steamclient.so
# (~/.steam/sdk64) at runtime.
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$PWD:$PWD/.steam/sdk64:${LD_LIBRARY_PATH:-}"
echo "[refx] launching: $BIN ${ARGS[*]}"
# Capture the server's own stdout/stderr so a startup crash is visible (Arma is
# otherwise silent until the .rpt appears).
"$BIN" "${ARGS[@]}" > "$PWD/arma3-console.log" 2>&1 &
SRV=$!
tail -n +1 -F "$PWD/arma3-console.log" 2>/dev/null &

# Follow the newest RPT to stdout, re-targeting when Arma rotates the file.
(
  last=
  while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do
    f="$(find "$PROFILES" -type f -name '*.rpt' -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -n1 | cut -d' ' -f2-)"
    if [ -n "$f" ] && [ "$f" != "$last" ]; then
      last="$f"
      echo "[refx] streaming Arma 3 log: ${f##*/}"
      tail -n +1 -F "$f" 2>/dev/null &
    fi
    sleep 2
  done
) &

# Wait for the server to exit, surviving the SIGTERM that triggers graceful stop.
while kill -0 "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; do wait "$SRV" 2>/dev/null; done
RC=$?
echo "[refx] arma3server exited (code $RC)."
echo "[refx] --- last server stdout/stderr ---"
tail -n 80 "$PWD/arma3-console.log" 2>/dev/null
RPT="$(find "$PROFILES" -type f -name '*.rpt' -printf '%T@ %p\n' 2>/dev/null | sort -rn | head -n1 | cut -d' ' -f2-)"
if [ -n "$RPT" ]; then
  echo "[refx] --- last lines of ${RPT##*/} ---"
  tail -n 50 "$RPT" 2>/dev/null
else
  echo "[refx] (no .rpt written — crash was before Arma init; the stderr above is the reason. A code 139 here = missing/incompatible steamclient.so)"
fi
RUNEOF
chmod +x /mnt/server/refx-run.sh
refx_steam_verify "$REFX_APPID"
echo 'Arma 3 install complete.'

variables.description: Name shown in the in-game server browser.
Leave blank for a public server.

longDescription: Run large-scale milsim operations with full mod support on a dedicated Arma 3 server.
